decision.archive_summary: The user asked to add repeatable in-repo smoke/eval coverage for Codex Context Minor GC, including harder natural Chinese multi-turn topic-switch cases, and explicitly preferred non-whitelist/non-hardcoded behavior. The assistant reported adding fixed live eval cases, broadening allowed guarded relations to include `continue`, and tightening the packaged guard so it cannot override a shadow-guard veto. The last resolved action was rerunning the live eval command and reporting it as passed.
decision.reasoning_summary: The active request in the transcript was completed by the assistant, so the conversation state is mostly resolved rather than continuing an open task. Older turns describe now-resolved implementation and status updates, making them good raw-eviction candidates. The durable parts worth preserving as semantic pins are the user's regression expectations and anti-whitelist preference, plus the assistant's compact summary of the guard constraint, while the raw evidence can leave the next-turn prompt.
